meta.title: SpeedyCAT Practice Question Bank — scraped sample set
meta.description: MCAT-format practice questions collected by scraping openly/freely-licensed web sources. Built to match the PRD `PracticeQuestion` data model, plus a `passage`/`passageId` extension for passage-based items.
meta.disclaimer: Exploratory sample set. NOT from AAMC full-length practice tests. No AAMC-originated content is included. Every item carries traceable source metadata. Items whose license is proprietary/unclear are explicitly flagged with sourceLicense containing 'LICENSE-UNCERTAIN' and a note; do not ship those without clearing rights.

extensions.sourceUrl: Direct URL of the scraped page (traceability; PRD requires source attribution).
extensions.passage: Full passage text for passage-based items (CARS). Null/omitted for discrete questions.
extensions.passageId: Shared id grouping questions that hang off the same passage. Null/omitted for discretes.

schemaNotes.choices: Array of 4 objects {label: A|B|C|D, text}. correctAnswer is the matching label letter.

questions.stem: What is the definition of a Brønsted-Lowry acid?
questions.explanation: A Brønsted-Lowry acid is defined as a proton (H+) donor; the species that accepts the proton is the Brønsted-Lowry base. (Electron-pair donor/acceptor are the Lewis definitions.)
questions.sourceName: LibreTexts — General, Organic, and Biochemistry with Problems, Case Studies, and Activities (Roosevelt University), Ch. 9 Acids and Bases
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim. The course's odd-answer key page (16.9) did not render during scraping; this definitional answer is independently verified.

questions.stem: What is the conjugate base of HF?
questions.explanation: Removing a proton (H+) from HF leaves F-, which is its conjugate base.
questions.sourceName: LibreTexts — General, Organic, and Biochemistry with Problems, Case Studies, and Activities (Roosevelt University), Ch. 9 Acids and Bases
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim; answer independently verified (definitional).

questions.stem: What pH does an acidic solution have?

choices.label: D
choices.text: The definition of acidity depends on the molecule.

questions.explanation: At 25 °C pure (neutral) water has pH 7. Acidic solutions have [H3O+] greater than 1.0×10^-7 M, which corresponds to pH < 7.
questions.sourceName: LibreTexts — General, Organic, and Biochemistry with Problems, Case Studies, and Activities (Roosevelt University), Ch. 9 Acids and Bases
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim; answer independently verified (definitional).

questions.stem: What is the pH of a solution with [H3O+] = 4.1 × 10^-5 M?
questions.sourceName: LibreTexts — General, Organic, and Biochemistry with Problems, Case Studies, and Activities (Roosevelt University), Ch. 9 Acids and Bases
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim; answer independently verified by calculation.

questions.stem: Which of the following is a nonpolar (hydrophobic) amino acid?
questions.explanation: Valine has a branched hydrocarbon (isopropyl) side chain and is nonpolar/hydrophobic. Serine (–OH) and tyrosine (phenol) are polar, and lysine is positively charged.
questions.sourceName: LibreTexts — General, Organic, and Biochemistry with Problems, Case Studies, and Activities (Roosevelt University), Ch. 14 Proteins
questions.notes: correctAnswer from the course's Chapter 16 answer key (16.14: Q1 = c).

questions.stem: Which of the following elements is not found in amino acids?
questions.explanation: The standard amino acids are built from carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, and nitrogen (with sulfur in cysteine and methionine). Phosphorus is not part of the amino acid structure itself.
questions.sourceName: LibreTexts — General, Organic, and Biochemistry with Problems, Case Studies, and Activities (Roosevelt University), Ch. 14 Proteins
questions.notes: correctAnswer from the course's Chapter 16 answer key (16.14: Q2 = b).

questions.stem: Hydrogen bonding is most important in stabilizing the ________ structure of many proteins.
questions.explanation: Secondary structure (α-helices and β-pleated sheets) is held together mainly by hydrogen bonds between backbone carbonyl (C=O) and amide (N–H) groups.
questions.sourceName: LibreTexts — General, Organic, and Biochemistry with Problems, Case Studies, and Activities (Roosevelt University), Ch. 14 Proteins
questions.notes: correctAnswer from the course's Chapter 16 answer key (16.14: Q13 = b).

questions.stem: The ________ is the basic unit of life.
questions.explanation: Cell theory holds that the cell is the basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Biology 2e (hosted on Biology LibreTexts), 2.1 Cell Structure — Review Questions
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim. OpenStax withholds the official answer key publicly; this answer is independently verified from the same CC BY source text.

questions.stem: Which of the following organisms is a prokaryote?
questions.explanation: E. coli is a bacterium and therefore a prokaryote. Amoeba and charophyte algae are eukaryotes, and a virus is not a cellular organism.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Biology 2e (hosted on Biology LibreTexts), 2.1 Cell Structure — Review Questions
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim. OpenStax withholds the official answer key publicly; answer independently verified from the same CC BY source text.

questions.stem: Which of the following is not a component of the endomembrane system?
questions.explanation: The endomembrane system includes the nuclear envelope, ER, Golgi apparatus, lysosomes, vesicles, and plasma membrane. Mitochondria are not part of it (they have their own double membrane and likely arose endosymbiotically).
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Biology 2e (hosted on Biology LibreTexts), 2.1 Cell Structure — Review Questions
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim. OpenStax withholds the official answer key publicly; answer independently verified from the same CC BY source text.

questions.stem: Which of the following is most likely to have the greatest concentration of smooth endoplasmic reticulum?

choices.label: C
choices.text: a cell that makes steroid hormones

choices.label: D
choices.text: a cell that engages in photosynthesis

questions.explanation: Smooth ER synthesizes lipids, including steroids, so steroid-hormone–producing cells (e.g., in the gonads or adrenal cortex) are especially rich in smooth ER. Enzyme (protein) secretion relies on rough ER.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Biology 2e (hosted on Biology LibreTexts), 2.1 Cell Structure — Review Questions
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim. OpenStax withholds the official answer key publicly; answer independently verified from the same CC BY source text.

questions.stem: To which of the following classes of sugars does glucose belong?
questions.explanation: Glucose is a six-carbon sugar, so it is a hexose.
questions.sourceName: LibreTexts — General, Organic, and Biochemistry with Problems, Case Studies, and Activities (Roosevelt University), Ch. 12 Carbohydrates
questions.notes: correctAnswer from the course's Chapter 16 answer key (16.12: Q7 = b).

questions.stem: Which of the following is a disaccharide?
questions.explanation: Lactose is a disaccharide of glucose and galactose. Galactose and fructose are monosaccharides, and amylose is a polysaccharide (a component of starch).
questions.sourceName: LibreTexts — General, Organic, and Biochemistry with Problems, Case Studies, and Activities (Roosevelt University), Ch. 12 Carbohydrates
questions.notes: correctAnswer from the course's Chapter 16 answer key (16.12: Q8 = b).

questions.stem: A carbohydrate with a C=O bond at carbon 2 would be classified as a(n)
questions.explanation: A carbonyl (C=O) on an internal carbon such as C2 is a ketone group, making the sugar a ketose. An aldose has its carbonyl at C1 (an aldehyde). Triose/hexose describe carbon count, not carbonyl position.
questions.sourceName: LibreTexts — General, Organic, and Biochemistry with Problems, Case Studies, and Activities (Roosevelt University), Ch. 12 Carbohydrates
questions.notes: correctAnswer from the course's Chapter 16 answer key (16.12: Q3 = b).

questions.stem: In the scientific method, a hypothesis is a(n) ________.
questions.explanation: A hypothesis is a tentative, testable proposed explanation for an observed phenomenon, generated before data are collected.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Psychology 2e, Ch. 1 Review Questions
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solution Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 1 Q3 = D.

questions.stem: ________ is most well-known for proposing his hierarchy of needs.
questions.explanation: Abraham Maslow proposed the hierarchy of needs, a humanistic motivation theory ascending from physiological needs to self-actualization.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Psychology 2e, Ch. 1 Review Questions
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solution Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 1 Q5 = C.

questions.stem: The operant conditioning chamber (aka ________ box) is a device used to study the principles of operant conditioning.
questions.explanation: The operant conditioning chamber is commonly called the 'Skinner box,' after B. F. Skinner, who used it to study how reinforcement and punishment shape behavior.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Psychology 2e, Ch. 1 Review Questions
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solution Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 1 Q7 = A.

questions.stem: An individual's consistent pattern of thought and behavior is known as a(n) ________.
questions.explanation: Personality is defined as an individual's consistent, characteristic pattern of thinking, feeling, and behaving.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Psychology 2e, Ch. 1 Review Questions
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solution Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 1 Q9 = C.

questions.stem: Which of the following best describes sociology as a subject?

choices.label: C
choices.text: The study of society and social interaction

questions.explanation: Sociology is the systematic study of society and social interaction—how groups, institutions, and social forces shape human behavior.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 1 Section Quiz
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 1 Q1 = C.

questions.stem: Who believed that the history of society was one of class struggle?
questions.explanation: Karl Marx argued that society's history is fundamentally a history of class struggle between those who own the means of production and those who labor for them.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 1 Section Quiz
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 1 Q9 = B.

questions.stem: A symbolic interactionist may compare social interactions to:
questions.explanation: Symbolic interactionism (via Goffman's dramaturgical analysis) compares social interaction to a theatrical performance in which people play roles on a social 'stage.'
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 1 Section Quiz
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 1 Q11 = D.
